mnemonics.title: GATDC - Shared Memory Call Order
mnemonics.content: Get, Attach, Touch, Detach, Control -- shmget(), shmat(), use memory, shmdt(), shmctl(). Remember: you must Get the key first, Attach to use, and always Detach and Control when done.

mnemonics.title: P and V - Semaphore Operations
mnemonics.content: P for Proberen (Dutch for 'test') = Wait/Decrement. V for Verhogen (Dutch for 'raise') = Signal/Increment. Think: P = Pass through (wait) and V = Vacate (release).

mnemonics.title: MEF - Producer-Consumer Semaphore Init
mnemonics.content: Mutex=1, Empty=BufferSize, Full=0. The initial values for the three semaphores. MEF helps you remember the starting configuration.

mnemonics.title: FISK - Four IPC Types
mnemonics.content: Files (pipes/FIFOs), IPC keys (ftok), Shared memory, Kernel persistence. Remember System V IPC objects persist in the kernel until explicitly removed.

mnemonics.title: RACE - Why Synchronization Matters
mnemonics.content: Read-And-Change-Error: Without synchronization, concurrent Read-And-Change operations on shared memory lead to Errors (race conditions).
